commit.message:
feat(modules): add incident-diag agent — structured RCA with 6 diagnostic tools

New files:
- platform/tools/diag_tools.py: 6 BaseTool subclasses (diag_logs, diag_process_stats,
  diag_db_stats, diag_endpoint_latency, diag_queue_depth, diag_correlate)
- skills/incident-diagnosis.md: RCA workflow skill (collect→correlate→hypothesise→confirm→recommend)

Wired:
- platform/agents/tool_runner.py: incident-diag module guard + register_diag_tools
- platform/agents/tool_schemas.py: diagnostic role in ROLE_TOOL_MAP (read-only tools only)
- platform/modules/registry.yaml: incident-diag module entry (25 modules total, category: ops)

Design choices:
- Read-only: no code_write, no deploys — investigation only, never modifies production state
- diag_correlate returns a structured PROMPT, not computed output — RCA is reasoning, not maths
- All tools fail-open: missing psutil/httpx/pg handled gracefully with install hints
- diagnostic role includes error-monitoring read tools + perf-audit tools (if module enabled)
  so one agent runs a complete incident investigation without tool-hopping

Source: original — inspired by Google SRE handbook, Netflix Chaos Engineering, PagerDuty runbooks

commit.message:
Improve performance: slices, partial-read pre-filter, larger I/O buffer

- Replace container/list with []string in SizeMap and DuplicateMap:
  reduces per-file heap allocations and improves cache locality
- Add FilterByPartialHash phase: reads only the first 4 KB of each
  candidate file before the full MD5 pass; files that differ in the
  first 4 KB cannot be duplicates, so they are skipped entirely.
  Dramatically reduces I/O for large files with many same-size peers.
- Use io.CopyBuffer with a 1 MB buffer (via sync.Pool) for full
  checksumming, reducing syscall overhead on large files
- Update verbose timing output to show the new partial-hash phase

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

never cause panics, undefined behavior, or safety violations across
all supported device vendors.

Crates with new proptest harnesses:
- hid-fanatec-protocol: standard, extended, pedal parsers
- hid-simucube-protocol: HID report, input report, steering bounds
- hid-asetek-protocol: parse never panics, derived finite, truncation
- hid-cammus-protocol: parse never panics, axes bounded, truncation
- hid-openffboard-protocol: parse, steering, button safety, truncation
- hid-ffbeast-protocol: parse, parse_with_id, normalized, wrong ID
- hid-heusinkveld-protocol: parse, normalized finite, truncation
- hid-button-box-protocol: gamepad, extended, axis, button, truncation
- hid-moza-protocol: HBP, SRP, any PID, axes bounded
- hid-thrustmaster-protocol: enhanced with parse_input, parse_pedal,
  axes_bounded, truncation tests

Cross-vendor integration test:
- New hid_cross_vendor_fuzz_tests.rs feeds random bytes to all 14+
  parser functions simultaneously, including edge cases (empty, single

test: add device protocol parsing snapshot tests for Fanatec, Simagic, VRS, and Moza (#162)

Add comprehensive snapshot/golden-file tests for input report parsing
across all four HID protocol crates using insta. Tests cover:

- Fanatec: standard/extended/pedal report parsing, inverted pedal axes,
  12-bit Hall sensor masking, protocol version detection, VID/PID
  category matrix, rim/wheelbase/pedal capability matrices (16 tests)
- Simagic: firmware version detection from byte offsets, all gear
  positions, shifter flag combinations, half-press pedal axes, quick
  release states, model-from-PID matrix, vendor ID constants (9 tests)
- VRS: connection detection (all-FF steering), quarter-range values,
  button bitmask parsing, signed encoder values, vendor ID, full device
  identity matrix (7 tests)
- Moza: wheelbase aggregated input report parsing, pedal axes extraction,
  minimal report threshold, V1/V2 PID pattern verification, device
  identity for all known PIDs, model torque matrix, rim ID constants,
  ES compatibility matrix (11 tests)

All tests use Result-returning signatures (no unwrap/expect) per project
conventions. Snapshot files are committed alongside test sources.

* .NET: Fixing issue where OpenTelemetry span is never exported in .NET in-process workflow execution (#4196)

* 1. Add reproduction test for issue #4155: workflow.run Activity never stopped in streaming OffThread path

The WorkflowRunActivity_IsStopped_Streaming_OffThread test demonstrates that
the workflow.run OpenTelemetry Activity created in StreamingRunEventStream.RunLoopAsync
is started but never stopped when using the OffThread/Default streaming execution.
The background run loop keeps running after event consumption completes, so the
using Activity? declaration never disposes until explicit StopAsync() is called.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

2. Fix workflow.run Activity never stopped in streaming OffThread execution (#4155)

The workflow.run OpenTelemetry Activity in StreamingRunEventStream.RunLoopAsync
was scoped to the method lifetime via 'using'. Since the run loop only exits on
cancellation, the Activity was never stopped/exported until explicit disposal.

Fix: Remove 'using' and explicitly dispose the Activity when the workflow reaches
Idle status (all supersteps complete). A safety-net disposal in the finally block
handles cancellation and error paths.

commit.message:
feat(tls_certificate): add `mittwald_tls_certificate` resource (#343)

Wildcard TLS certificates must be provisioned separately via the
mittwald API since they require DNS validation and cannot be implicitly
created with a virtual host. This adds a `mittwald_tls_certificate`
resource to manage that lifecycle. Additionally, the resource supports
importing pre-existing PEM-encoded certificates and private keys.

## Changes

- **New resource `mittwald_tls_certificate`**
(`internal/provider/resource/tlscertificateresource/`)
  - Supports two creation modes:
- **DNS validation**: provide `common_name` + `project_id` to request a
DNS-validated certificate (suitable for wildcard domains like
`*.foobar.example`)
- **Certificate import**: provide `certificate`, `private_key_wo`,
`private_key_wo_version`, and optionally `common_name` to import a
pre-existing PEM-encoded certificate
  - Create blocks (up to 30 min) with a two-phase polling strategy:
    1. Polls `ssl-get-certificate-request` until `isCompleted=true`
2. Finds the issued certificate via `ssl-list-certificates` by matching
`certificateRequestId`
3. For DNS-validated certificates, additionally polls
`ssl-get-certificate` until `dnsCertSpec.status.status == "ready"`;
terminal error states (`error`, `cnameError`) are surfaced immediately
- Imported certificates (no `DnsCertSpec`) skip the readiness polling
step
- Both `certificate_request_id` and `id` (certificate) are persisted in
state
- `common_name` is Optional+Computed — required for DNS validation,
auto-derived from the certificate CN for imports
- `private_key_wo` is a write-only attribute; the private key is re-sent
to the API when `private_key_wo_version` changes or `certificate`
changes
- In-place certificate renewal via `ReplaceCertificate` when
`certificate` or `private_key_wo_version` changes
- Switching between DNS validation and certificate import modes forces
replacement
  - Delete cleans up both the certificate and the certificate request
  - Supports `terraform import` by certificate ID
- **Config validation**: `certificate`, `private_key_wo`, and
`private_key_wo_version` must all be set together or not at all;
`common_name` is required when none of the import fields are set (DNS
validation mode)

- **Registered** the new resource in `provider.go`

- **Examples**:
- `examples/resources/mittwald_tls_certificate/resource.tf` — DNS
validation (wildcard cert + `mittwald_virtualhost` with `depends_on`)
- `examples/resources/mittwald_tls_certificate/resource_import.tf` —
certificate import from PEM files with `private_key_wo`

- **README** updated to include `mittwald_tls_certificate` in the
resources list

- **Docs** regenerated via `go generate`
(`docs/resources/tls_certificate.md`)

## Usage

### DNS validation (wildcard)

```hcl
resource "mittwald_tls_certificate" "wildcard" {
  project_id  = var.project_id
  common_name = "*.foobar.example"
}

resource "mittwald_virtualhost" "example" {
  project_id = var.project_id
  hostname   = "app.foobar.example"
  paths      = { "/" = {} }

  depends_on = [mittwald_tls_certificate.wildcard]
}
```

### Certificate import

```hcl
resource "mittwald_tls_certificate" "imported" {
  project_id = var.project_id

  certificate            = file("${path.module}/cert.pem")
  private_key_wo         = file("${path.module}/key.pem")
  private_key_wo_version = 1
}

resource "mittwald_virtualhost" "example" {
  project_id = var.project_id
  hostname   = "foo.example"
  paths      = { "/" = {} }

  depends_on = [mittwald_tls_certificate.imported]
}
```

<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Create a `mittwald_certificate` resource</issue_title>
> <issue_description>_Follow-Up from #340_
> 
> ---
> 
> Wildcard TLS certificates cannot be created implicitly together with a
Virtual Host, but must be requested separately using the
[`ssl-create-certificate-request` API
operation](https://developer.mittwald.de/docs/v2/reference/domain/ssl-create-certificate-request/).
> 
> ## Solution concept
> 
> 1. Add a `mittwald_tls_certificate` resource that can be used as
follows:
> 
>     ```hcl
>     resource "mittwald_tls_certificate" "wildcard_example" {
>         common_name = "*.foobar.example"
>         project_id = var.project_id
>     }
>     ```
> 
> 2. During creation, the [`ssl-get-certificate-request`
operation](https://developer.mittwald.de/docs/v2/reference/domain/ssl-get-certificate-request/)
must be polled repeatedly (using the ID returned by
`ssl-create-certificate-request`) until `isCompleted` is true.
> 
> 3. Both certificate request ID and certificate ID should be persisted
in the resource state. The certificate (unlike the certificate request)
may need to be searched in the result list of the
`ssl-list-certificates` operation.
> 
> 3. After that, the certificate may be used
> 
> 4. Certificates are used automatically for virtual hosts. The examples
should include a `mittwald_virtualhost` resource used together with a
`mittwald_tls_certificate`, connected by `depends_on` with appropriate
documentation.</issue_description>
